feed.title: AO3 - Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne (Filtered)
feed.subtitle: Latest works for Clark Kent/Bruce Wayne on Archive of Our Own [English, No ABO]

entry.title: mourning the lilac gardens
entry.summary:
Author: cinderellion

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 40,715Chapters: 4/4Kudos: 576

Summary: His body stilled. The two flimsy wrist bands felt heavy in Damian's calloused hands. Everyone was just as entranced in horror over the two tiny little things in the youngest hands. Two wristbands with the name Bruce Wayne, belonging to Arkham Asylum. _______ Or, Two times Bruce Wayne went to Arkham Asylum, and the time his kids found out.

entry.title: The Gotham Years
entry.summary:
Author: x_starfry

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,765Chapters: 1/45Kudos: 0

Summary: Damian al Ghul was raised to be a weapon. Then Bruce Wayne brought him home to a manor full of vigilantes, unresolved trauma, and far too many people willing to love him anyway. A series of one-shots following Damian’s childhood — from terrifyingly tiny assassin baby to Gotham’s smallest Robin.   Featuring: accidental family bonding Bruce Wayne panic-parenting tiny League-trained behaviors Batfamily chaos emotional damage healing Alfred running the entire household and one very small child slowly realizing he is safe Updates whenever inspiration strikes. (very often)

entry.title: Seven minutes in heaven
entry.summary:
Author: SubtleSolstice

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 259,222Chapters: 39/?Kudos: 865

Summary: Dick Grayson thought it would be harmless, one chill night where the Young Justice crew and the Titans could actually relax together. No missions. No explosions. Just friends, laughter… and maybe a little too much alcohol. But when a round of Never Have I Ever spirals into Seven Minutes in Heaven, Dick and Wally find themselves a little too close in the dark — and suddenly, nothing feels simple anymore. Or;Wally and Dick’s developing relationship, full of tension, tenderness, and the two of them absolutely failing to keep their hands to themselves.

entry.title: Assignations
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 35,178Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 9

Summary: Saul DiGennaro has been in this life 32 years. He knows what the rules cost and who pays them and what happens to men who forget they agreed to pay. He also knows what money looks like when it moves wrong. What a city looks like when the thing underneath it has been there longer than the families and will be there after them. He's been telling a reporter named Clark Kent about it, in diners and bars and one significantly better coffee place, across eight months of conversations that are not on the record and then are and then aren't again depending on the specific weight of what's being said. He did two weeks in Gotham County. He came out intact. What he heard in there — from women transferring through from state units with no running water and mess halls that ran out of food and guards who waited three hours while a woman seized — that's in the notebook now. He keeps running into Matches Malone in alleys and stairwells and the back seats of Suburbans and one supply closet during an active heist, and Matches keeps having what appears to be a recurring and energetic arrangement with Tony the Ace, and Saul keeps filing it under the drawer that doesn't have a label yet.

  • Creator Chose Not To Use Archive Warnings

entry.title: Undisclosed Desires
entry.summary:
Author: lmaosobsquietly

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 8,215Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 72

Summary: Clark Kent has a habit of seeing the good in people. It’s what makes him a great reporter—and what makes Bruce Wayne a problem. Bruce doesn’t make sense. Not as a careless billionaire, not as the man Clark meets in quieter moments, and certainly not as the person Clark finds himself thinking about long after their conversations end. Bruce Wayne didn't plan on falling for a journalist with too many questions, and a smile that sees right through him. Bruce knows exactly why Clark is confused. He knows Clark is getting close to something he shouldn’t see, asking questions that don’t have safe answers. It’s a dangerous kind of attention—one Bruce should shut down immediately. Instead, he lets Clark in. Slowly. Carefully. Too much. As their connection deepens into something softer, something undeniable, the truth looms between them. AKA: First meet Bruce and Clark; their connection grows deeper as time goes along.

entry.title: Cantos of the Damned: A SuperBat Retelling of Dante's Inferno
entry.summary:
Author: EllEmmeFujo

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,342Chapters: 5/?Kudos: 8

Summary: A bet is placed. Batman is dead and finds himself in hell. With Constantine as his guide, he must find the person he treasures most and save them...even if it means damning himself to an eternity of fire and brimstone.

entry.title: Shadow and Sun
entry.summary:
Author: Katylark

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 43,664Chapters: 4/25Kudos: 5

Summary: When Darkseid’s invasion forces Earth’s heroes to unite, an unknown alien changes the course of the battle—and the world names him Superman. Eighteen months later, Superman is beloved across the globe, distrusted by Batman, and lonelier than anyone knows. Clark Kent has lost both of his parents, built a Fortress in the Arctic, and is quietly coming undone under the weight of grief, secrecy, and the strange Kryptonian bonds that once made his family feel like home. Then he meets Bruce Wayne. What begins across the waters between Metropolis and Gotham as an unexpected romance becomes something deeper, more dangerous, and far more fragile than either of them is prepared for—especially when Clark’s last surviving family falls from the stars, Lex Luthor starts asking all the right questions, and Bruce mistakes the truth for betrayal. A story about first trust, second chances, alien invasions, black sites, the family you lose, and the family you choose.

entry.title: EFFORTS TO REVERSE THE FLOODING OF GOTHAM / Myotis septentrionalis; behaviors and observations
entry.summary:
Author: Save_Feza

Rating: Not RatedLanguage: EnglishWords: 31,693Chapters: 3/?Kudos: 38

Summary: In the aftermath of Lex Luthor’s attacks, Clark has been attempting to find the good in all the distress. Luckily, down the road in Gotham, the restoration efforts after similar disasters may inspire the people of Metropolis to look to the skies with hope and ambition again. The hard part is that his source, one Bruce Wayne, isn’t the sociable type. Good thing Superman has the means to befriend Gotham's protector, Batman!Bruce Wayne is struggling to find his place in the reconstruction of Gotham. It doesn't help that in the confusing swirl of spearheading a new construction company, stopping a new rampant drug plaguing the streets, and potentially adopting a child, an overly friendly reporter will not leave him alone. Not to mention the sudden appearance of Superman.–This probably sucks but at least I'm not using ai :)Shoutout to my friends who gave me the go ahead to start publishing! UPDATES WILL BE SLOW I AM STARTING YEAR 3 OF COLLEGE

entry.title: Kryptonian Reproduction, an Observation
entry.summary:
Author: Winkaku

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 92,950Chapters: 31/31Kudos: 1,823

Summary: They'd been caught in each others orbit for some time now and, quite frankly, Clark should have seen this coming. Crazy was part and parcel to Gotham after all... let alone his own life. His father had warned him about mad scientists, but Clark had the distinct feeling that this was not what he'd envisioned in the slightest.AKA: A series of PWP vignettes wherein the plot is entirely background and I burn through kinks faster than a sorority freshman. Seriously, I’m running out of ideas, leave a comment with a suggestion and I’ll see what I can do.AKA: That time I tried to make a kinky science fic that turned into a writing challenge that turned into a crazy romp with associated art pieces. Fun was had by all.

entry.title: What Becomes of the Unready
entry.summary:
Author: NewYorke

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 5,878Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 69

Summary: “When a fledgling leaves the nest, there isn’t much the parent can do. They gave it warmth, shelter, and every scrap of knowledge they knew. But there comes a point where the sky calls, and the chick must answer alone. It will either follow its instincts and take flight, or plummet into the jaws of a world hungry without mercy.” —--- You’d been on plenty of watches in the rain, knew how to keep your footing while leaping from rooftop to rooftop. This was Gotham, after all–the city had its own kind of plot armor, keeping the streets as sullen as humanly possible. To have a patrol without rain was to have coffee without sugar.   Tonight, though, you were flying solo. Bruce would’ve permanently benched you if he knew, but honestly… whatever. You helped solved cases before, peeked at the cams. How different could it be? You could *see* Bruce from where you were anyways. It was a little harmless shadowing, what he didn’t know couldn’t hurt him. You’d slip back into the manor before they started to head back. Besides, the team was still nearby, somewhere in the shadows.   Aka: Joker finally catches a chick the moment it drops from the nest

entry.title: This is a love story…. Of a detective and a news reporter.
entry.summary:
Author: TheLexLuther

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 12,109Chapters: 15/?Kudos: 1

Summary: This is a love story between play boy billionaire Bruce Wayne and news reporter Clark Kent fall in love after one visit to metropolis. Bruce is visiting (metropolis and Gotham are right next to each other in this fic, it’ll make sense later) for a circus show later that week and a charity gala. He ends up adopts a child name Richard (dick) Grayson which ends up having a weird attachment to Clark Kent. Bruce and Clark always had a weird relationship with one another. Clark hates Bruce. Thinks he’s to much of a play boy, but loves Batman who he thinks cares so much for his city. While Bruce has a small crush on Clark Kent, the shy but very well built news reporter, but hates Superman…just for his own personal reasons. This story is how they build their Superbat family.

entry.title: World's Finest
entry.summary:
Author: aeej

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 3,101Chapters: 2/?Kudos: 31

Summary: Clark Kent was sent with clear instructions: Get a statement from Bruce Wayne. Yet, predictably, Bruce is late. He’d long come to terms with facing Perry tomorrow as a failure, but refused to leave here empty-handed. However, this left him standing defeatedly attempting to comprehend the scene of garbled scribbles before him.  He tilted his head, pulling a face, feeling bested by A trombone, maybe? He sighed, adjusting his glasses as he raised his recorder. “Uh… ‘Femme… et… uh, boy sons?” He attempted, for his own clarification, later. “Portrays a witch woman…wailing over a broken trombone.” He whinged, tapping his recorder against his chin with an added “Real hard-hitting journalism, Clark,” Before disheartenedly shutting it off. He’d long tuned out the hushed whispers and illicit business affairs the groups had divided into, due to the unethical source of information and primarily to maintain his own willful ignorance.  Which is how he must’ve missed the approach of the man behind him. “That’s a bit harsh, no?”

entry.title: Bats in the Attic
entry.summary:
Author: Anonymous

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 6,293Chapters: 3/5Kudos: 92

Summary: Where he came from or what his motives were weren't immediately clear. He and the other Kryptonians attacked out of nowhere. At first, Bruce suspected some sort of mind control. But when Kal-El swatted Bruce's green kryptonite out of his hand like it was a child's toy, laughing and unaffected, he knew it was something deeper than that. But his contingencies failed, one after another. Bruce failed. Kal-El brought him, beaten half to death, here. Locked him in this lonely cell… and nursed him back to health. Almost tenderly. He's healed now, but no more able to escape then when he was to injured too move. He tells himself he's biding his time, waiting for an opportunity to escape. To fight back. For Kal-El to make a mistake that he can use. In the meantime, Kal-El comes to him every night.

entry.title: Box of Chocolates
entry.summary:
Author: unbreakabledawn

Rating: Teen And Up AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 2,130Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 38

Summary: My sources tell me, that across the universe, there are stories to be found where a very tiny person loves a very big person. It's me. I'm my source. (A series of unconnected one-shots, flash fics, and prompt fills centered around Superbat and Giant/Tiny.)

entry.title: Parent Trap
entry.summary:
Author: i8thecookie

Rating: General AudiencesLanguage: EnglishWords: 36,992Chapters: 13/15Kudos: 693

Summary: When Dick Grayson forces Clark to pretend to be his parent for a meeting with his headmaster, Clark expects an awkward conversation and maybe a lecture from Bruce afterward. He does not expect Gotham’s tabloids to decide he and Bruce Wayne are secretly together. But after one too many headlines about Bruce Wayne’s “mysterious co-parent,” pretending to date starts seeming like the easiest solution. That would probably be less of a problem if Clark wasn’t already half in love with him.

  • No Archive Warnings Apply

entry.title: and when i'm gone, just carry on, don't mourn, rejoice
entry.summary:
Author: dwarflover4life

Rating: MatureLanguage: EnglishWords: 19,501Chapters: 8/?Kudos: 151

Summary: He wanted to end it – had thought about doing it since his parents had passed when he was just an eight-year-old boy – but he couldn’t. When he felt this way he often thought of his oldest son, Dick, and his butler Alfred. And Clark. And that’s why he’s decided to draft up a new will and a contingency plan of sorts. He already had many in place, but this would be the final one – he needed to make it count. So that when he ended it all, everything would go smoothly, and they could all forget about Bruce and finally be happy. It was the perfect solution. Everything could be fixed with a plan. At the start of January in the new year he would be a dead man.   (title inspired by 'When I'm Gone' by Eminem)

Summary: After a tragic concert incident, people begin killing themselves in Gotham. Wearing smiles.

entry.title: The New Son of Krypton
entry.summary:
Author: Sally_Boomer

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 14,100Chapters: 4/?Kudos: 444

Summary: Superman whipped the condensation on the healing-vessel window with a shaky hand, the oppression on his chest only growing more overwhelming the more he cleared the view. Then he saw it. There, floating in the fluid, was a foetus. “Kelex.” Kal’s voice came broken and strangled, and a little hysterical, “Kelex, what is inside?” “A Kryptonian-human, Kal-El.” Replied the AI, “A descendant designed to better adapt to the planet chosen to board the House of El. A new type of Kryptonian for a new planet.” “A hybrid for…” Started Kal, only to stop mid-sentence, suddenly assaulted by a dreadful realisation. “Which human? Whose DNA did you take for this? From whom did you take the human sample?” “Your chosen mate, Kal-El. Batman.” Kelex answered without pause, as if the answer was obvious. ---- Superman, who has been secretly in love with his best friend for over a decade, accidentally baby traps Batman with a human-kryptonian child. Or Clark Kent and Bruce Wayne are Conner Wayne's biological parents, and they may or may not fall in love while raising their child together.

entry.title: Kinæsthesia
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 96,618Chapters: 15/?Kudos: 158

Summary: "You are your own worst enemy."- Martha Kent Clark comes home to a city still wearing his symbol like a wound. Bruce comes home to Clark. It takes both of them longer than it should. Features: an opinionated elderly neighbor, a neighborhood that gives a damn, a word in Kryptonian that doesn't translate cleanly, and two men who are catastrophically bad at being afraid in front of each other. The longer version: After the Fortress, after the city's grief and the shaharreth on every wall, Clark Kent comes back to Metropolis and tries to figure out what home means for someone like him. Bruce Wayne is apparently part of that answer. Clark is working on accepting this. This is a story about the specific courage of choosing someone who will die before you do. About the difference between happiness and contentment. About the people who show up for you in your building hallway at six in the morning and the people who put a transponder in your cape because they can't say I was afraid for you any other way. Martha Kent called it. She usually does.

entry.title: Love Me For Forever
entry.summary:
Author: Holdt

Rating: ExplicitLanguage: EnglishWords: 57,080Chapters: 9/?Kudos: 15

Summary: Bruce Wayne died destroying a Xenomorph Queen. Clark Kent mourned him with Kryptonian ritual and carried the key to the Batcave against his sternum for nine days. Then Jason Todd brought Bruce back. Resurrection via Lazarus Pit is never clean. Bruce returns with his memories intact and a rage that displaces every other emotion. The two-meter distance that kept Clark alive in the hive becomes the boundary that keeps Bruce from violence. The team fractures over the ethics of resurrection without consent. And in the Pacific Northwest, something new is hunting—parasitic neural infiltrators that enter through the ear canal and leave their hosts conscious during control. Bruce volunteers as bait. Clark shadows him every meter. The key stays cold against Clark's chest. And somewhere in the Cascade Range, a Yautja marks Batman as worthy prey. "You were wrong to do it. But I'm glad you did." Love that persists despite impossibility. Love that consumes despite resistance. Love that exists in darkness and is overcome by forces larger than itself. This is what remains.
